subject: Mathematics
title: Symmetry in Nature
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Symmetry in Nature.

hook.text: Snowflakes have sixfold symmetry. Starfish have fivefold. Nature picks its favorites.

buildup.text: Water molecules bond at 120-degree angles, forcing ice crystals into hexagonal shapes.

discovery.text: Many flowers have 3, 5, or 8 petals—numbers from the Fibonacci sequence.

twist.text: Perfect symmetry is actually rare. Small defects make natural objects unique.

climax.text: From galaxies to DNA helixes, symmetry appears at every scale in the physical world.

punchline.text: Nature prefers patterns but never makes them perfect.

quiz.question: Why do snowflakes have sixfold symmetry?
options: Water molecules bond at angles that create hexagonal structures
They always have exactly six arms
